name: arbor
description: Maintain project-local Arbor memory, run deterministic framework checks for context and runtime hooks, and guide explicit Arbor workflow entrypoints from brainstorm, feedback, and converge; not a project-summary or health-advice command.

interface.shortDescription: Project memory plus explicit workflow skills
interface.longDescription: Arbor initializes project-local memory, registers executable project-level Codex hooks and runtime-specific hook wrappers, loads startup context in a stable order for fresh and resumed sessions, and runs deterministic framework checks with fixed rows for Category, Check, Status, Evidence, Fixability, and Repair action. The arbor skill is a detect-only framework check entrypoint by default, not a project-summary, project-status, resume-summary, or subjective health-advice skill; ordinary project overview answers should be direct and source-grounded after startup context is loaded, without selecting arbor unless initialization or an Arbor framework check is requested. Hook surfaces are reported separately for Codex project hooks, Claude plugin hooks, optional Claude project hooks, and runtime blockers. Brainstorm turns scoped requests, bug reports without review context, planning continuations, and broad workflow changes into reviewable plans with acceptance criteria and test scope before routing approved work to converge. Feedback accepts feedback-shaped bug reports, regressions, failed checks, reviewer comments, and corrections to prior Arbor work when the next public owner is unclear, then routes only to brainstorm, converge, needs-evidence, or a direct response; it does not trigger from keywords alone or intercept another named public entrypoint that already fits. Converge is the public quality-loop orchestrator for existing managed features: it handles bugs, defects, review findings, verification requests, finish/publish intent, and continuation requests, then drives the internal develop/evaluate cycle with release checkpoints before deciding whether the feature satisfies the plan. Develop, evaluate, and release are internal stages for implementation, independent validation, checkpoint commits, and post-convergence finalization evidence; they are not normal public user entrypoints, and users should not be asked to invoke release directly. Normal user-visible checkpoints render user_response and ui fields; raw workflow JSON, route labels, and terminal-state labels stay hidden unless the user explicitly asks for debug or machine output. Brainstorm and feedback checkpoints use their standard rendered headings, not ad hoc planning or routing prose.
defaultPrompt: Run an Arbor framework check for context, memory, and hooks
Use brainstorm for planning, feedback for unclear feedback, or converge for current-loop repair
